location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday; remote up to 4 weeks per year

level_signals: Expected to mentor PMs and elevate org-wide standards
Comp range $240K-$300K consistent with Staff-level IC at a growth-stage fintech
Defines multi-quarter product vision and strategy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7–10+ years of product management experience, with a demonstrated track record of leading complex, high-impact product initiatives in high-growth B2B or fintech environments.
Proven ability to define product strategy that translates into measurable business outcomes.
Strong fluency in technical systems and architecture. You are comfortable discussing APIs, data models, experimentation frameworks, and system tradeoffs with engineers.
Highly data-driven. Strong experience defining metrics, running experiments, and using SQL (or similar tools) to guide decisions.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. Ability to influence senior stakeholders through structured thinking and compelling narratives.
Ability to reason holistically across product, technical, operational, compliance, and revenue systems.
Demonstrated ability to raise the quality bar — for product thinking, execution, and customer experience.
Define and own a multi-quarter product vision and strategy aligned to Brex's highest company priorities.
Lead complex, cross-functional initiatives that span multiple teams, systems, and stakeholder groups.
You must be willing to work in the office at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building platform or financial infrastructure products is strongly preferred.

scope.surface: One of Brex's most critical product domains (e.g., financial infrastructure, risk systems, growth systems, payables ecosystem)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — corporate cards, banking, spend management, bill pay, travel software
core_responsibilities: Define and own a multi-quarter product vision and strategy aligned to Brex's highest company priorities
Identify high-leverage opportunities that create durable competitive advantage
Lead complex, cross-functional initiatives that span multiple teams, systems, and stakeholder groups
Develop deep domain expertise and use it to inform product direction
Partner closely with Engineering to design scalable technical systems
Set clear success metrics tied to company-level outcomes
Drive structured decision-making in ambiguous environments
Influence senior leaders and executives through clear thinking, strong written narratives, and data-backed recommendations
Improve product craft and operating standards across the organization, mentoring PMs

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and highly competitive ($240K-$300K base)
Clear product surface descriptions (financial infrastructure, risk systems, growth systems, payables ecosystem)
Explicit listing of cross-functional partners indicating organizational maturity
Brex has a well-known customer list and operates in 200+ markets — strong market signal
Role has genuine org-level influence — 'elevate standards of product thinking across the organization'
Responsibilities are specific and concrete rather than generic
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires 3 specific days/week (Mon, Wed, Thu) in SF, Seattle, or NYC — fully remote candidates ineligible
Remote allowed only up to 4 weeks/year minimum one week at a time — very restrictive
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not stated anywhere in the JD
'Strongly preferred' for platform/financial infrastructure is near-hard given the domain specificity of the role
7-10+ years range is wide; '10+' signal suggests ceiling expectations may be higher than floor

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years required, and org influence signals; 'Staff' is in job title)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false — role mentors PMs but no direct reports stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate's location')
ambiguities: Years range stated as '7–10+' which is unusual — could imply preference for 10+ years at Staff level; minimum treated as 7
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned anywhere in the JD; left as 'unclear'

Platform/financial infrastructure experience is labeled 'strongly preferred' which sits between hard and soft; classified as nice_to_have (soft) but is close to a hard requirement given the role's domain
